1612	\begin{lemma}[Half 2, seam 1 — finite type yields a finite cover of globally-generated affines]
1613	  \label{lem:gf_finiteType_affine_finite_cover_generated}
1614	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_finiteType_affine_finite_cover_generated}
1615	  % LEAN STATUS: base-case seam, project-built (Mathlib-absent). Proposed decl
1616	  % AlgebraicGeometry.gf_finiteType_affine_finite_cover_generated; extracts a
1617	  % standard finite cover of W on whose members F is globally generated, from the
1618	  % SheafOfModules.IsFiniteType local-generators datum refined by quasi-compactness
1619	  % of the affine W.
1620	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", Tag 01PB, proof
1621	  % (read from references/stacks-properties.tex, L2101-L2107).
1622	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
1623	  % "Assume $\widetilde M$ is a finite type $\mathcal{O}_X$-module. This means
1624	  %  there exists an open covering of $X$ such that $\widetilde M$ restricted to
1625	  %  the members of this covering is globally generated by finitely many sections.
1626	  %  Thus there also exists a standard open covering
1627	  %  $X = \bigcup_{i = 1, \ldots, n} D(f_i)$ such that $\widetilde M|_{D(f_i)}$
1628	  %  is generated by finitely many sections."
1629	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", Tag 01PB (standard
1630	  cover of generated affines).}
1631	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, \(\F\) a quasi-coherent sheaf of modules of finite type,
1632	  and \(W \subseteq X\) an affine open. Then there is a finite family
1633	  \(t \subseteq \Gamma(X, W)\) with \(\mathrm{Ideal.span}\,t = \top\) such that for
1634	  each \(g \in t\) the basic open \(D(g)\) is affine and \(\F|_{D(g)}\) is globally
1635	  generated by finitely many sections; equivalently, for each \(g \in t\) there is
1636	  a finite index set \(I_g\) and an epimorphism of \(\OO_{D(g)}\)-modules
1637	  \(\OO_{D(g)}^{\oplus I_g} \twoheadrightarrow \F|_{D(g)}\).
1638	\end{lemma}
1639	
1640	\begin{proof}
1641	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag 01PB (standard cover of generated affines).}
1642	  Finite type of \(\F\) means that \(X\) has an open covering on each member of
1643	  which \(\F\) is generated by finitely many sections. Intersecting this covering
1644	  with the affine \(W\) and refining the resulting cover of \(W\) by standard basic
1645	  opens, every point of \(W\) lies in a basic open \(D(g) \subseteq W\) on which
1646	  \(\F\) is generated by finitely many sections. Since \(W\) is affine, hence
1647	  quasi-compact, finitely many such basic opens \(D(g_1), \dots, D(g_n)\) already
1648	  cover \(W\); collect their defining elements into the finite family
1649	  \(t = \{g_1, \dots, g_n\} \subseteq \Gamma(X, W)\). That the \(D(g_i)\) cover
1650	  \(W = \Spec \Gamma(X, W)\) is exactly the condition
1651	  \(\mathrm{Ideal.span}\,t = \top\). A basic open of an affine is affine, and being
1652	  generated by a finite family of sections is precisely the existence of an
1653	  epimorphism from the corresponding finite free module. This is Stacks' passage
1654	  from the local-generation datum to a standard finite cover of generated affines.
1655	\end{proof}
1656	
1657	\begin{lemma}[Half 2, seam 2 — $\Gamma$ on affine quasi-coherent modules sends epis to surjections]
1658	  \label{lem:gf_affine_qcoh_Gamma_epi}
1659	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_affine_qcoh_Gamma_epi}
1660	  \uses{lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization}
1661	  % LEAN STATUS: base-case seam, project-built (Mathlib-absent). Proposed decl
1662	  % AlgebraicGeometry.gf_affine_qcoh_Gamma_epi. This is the precise descent input
1663	  % the base case relies on: across the gap1/gap2 affine qcoh ≃ Mod equivalence
1664	  % (M ≅ Γ(M,V)~ over V = Spec B), the section functor Γ corresponds to the exact
1665	  % forward functor, so a sheaf epimorphism descends to a surjective B-module map.
1666	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", Tag 01PB, proof
1667	  % (read from references/stacks-properties.tex, L2106-L2107) — the implication
1668	  % "$\widetilde M|_{D(f_i)}$ generated by finitely many sections $\Rightarrow$
1669	  % $M_{f_i}$ finitely generated" is exactly section-level surjectivity of a sheaf
1670	  % epi over an affine.
1671	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
1672	  % "such that $\widetilde M|_{D(f_i)}$ is generated by finitely many sections.
1673	  %  Thus $M_{f_i}$ is finitely generated for each $i$."
1674	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", Tag 01PB (sections of a
1675	  generated sheaf over an affine).}
1676	  Let \(V\) be an affine open of a scheme, with \(B = \Gamma(X, V)\) and
1677	  \(V \cong \Spec B\), and let \(\pi : \mathcal{G} \to \F\) be an epimorphism of
1678	  quasi-coherent sheaves of modules over \(V\). Then the induced map on sections
1679	  \(\Gamma(\pi) : \Gamma(\mathcal{G}, V) \to \Gamma(\F, V)\) is a surjective
1680	  \(B\)-module map.
1681	\end{lemma}
1682	
1683	\begin{proof}
1684	  \uses{lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization}
1685	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag 01PB (sections of a generated sheaf over an
1686	  affine).}
1687	  Over the affine \(V\) the section functor identifies quasi-coherent
1688	  \(\OO_V\)-modules with \(B\)-modules: each quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{M}\)
1689	  corresponds to its section module \(\Gamma(\mathcal{M}, V)\) via the
1690	  associated-sheaf equivalence \(\mathcal{M} \cong \widetilde{\Gamma(\mathcal{M}, V)}\),
1691	  the affine instance (\(U = \top\)) of the qcoh-sections descent
1692	  \cref{lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization}. Global sections of a quasi-coherent
1693	  sheaf on an affine are exact (the affine has no higher quasi-coherent cohomology),
1694	  so this equivalence carries the sheaf epimorphism \(\pi\) to a surjective
1695	  \(B\)-linear map; that map is \(\Gamma(\pi)\). Hence \(\Gamma(\pi)\) is surjective.
1696	\end{proof}

1698	\begin{lemma}[Half 2 base case — a globally-generated finite-type qcoh sheaf has finite affine sections]
1699	  \label{lem:gf_qcoh_finite_sections_globally_generated}
1700	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_qcoh_finite_sections_globally_generated}
1701	  \uses{lem:gf_affine_qcoh_Gamma_epi, lem:module_finite_of_surjective_mathlib}
1702	  % LEAN STATUS: base-case assembly, project-built (Mathlib-absent). Proposed decl
1703	  % AlgebraicGeometry.gf_qcoh_finite_sections_globally_generated. This is seam 2
1704	  % (Gamma-epi) followed by seam 3 (free surjection ⟹ finite). It feeds Half 1 with
1705	  % each per-member Module.Finite Γ(X,D(g)) Γ(F,D(g)).
1706	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", Tag 01PB
1707	  % (read from references/stacks-properties.tex, L2094-L2107).
1708	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1709	  % "Let $X = \Spec(R)$ be an affine scheme. The quasi-coherent sheaf of
1710	  %  $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules $\widetilde M$ is a finite type $\mathcal{O}_X$-module
1711	  %  if and only if $M$ is a finite $R$-module."
1712	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", Tag 01PB.}
1713	  Let \(V\) be an affine open of a scheme \(X\), write \(B = \Gamma(X, V)\), and let
1714	  \(\F\) be a quasi-coherent sheaf of modules. Suppose \(\F\) is globally generated
1715	  by finitely many sections on \(V\): there is a finite index set \(I\) and an
1716	  epimorphism of \(\OO_V\)-modules \(\OO_V^{\oplus I} \twoheadrightarrow \F|_V\).
1717	  Then \(\Gamma(\F, V)\) is a finite \(B\)-module.
1718	\end{lemma}
1719	
1720	\begin{proof}
1721	  \uses{lem:gf_affine_qcoh_Gamma_epi, lem:module_finite_of_surjective_mathlib}
1722	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag 01PB.}
1723	  Apply seam 2 (\cref{lem:gf_affine_qcoh_Gamma_epi}) to the epimorphism
1724	  \(\OO_V^{\oplus I} \twoheadrightarrow \F|_V\) over the affine \(V\): the section
1725	  functor yields a surjective \(B\)-module map
1726	  \(\Gamma(\OO_V^{\oplus I}, V) \to \Gamma(\F, V)\). The source is the sections of a
1727	  finite free \(\OO_V\)-module, so \(\Gamma(\OO_V^{\oplus I}, V) \cong B^{\oplus I}\)
1728	  is a finite free \(B\)-module. A finite module surjecting onto \(\Gamma(\F, V)\)
1729	  forces \(\Gamma(\F, V)\) to be finite, by
1730	  \cref{lem:module_finite_of_surjective_mathlib}. Hence \(\Gamma(\F, V)\) is a finite
1731	  \(B\)-module.
1732	\end{proof}

1734	\begin{lemma}[G1 — finite-type quasi-coherent sheaf has finite affine sections]
1735	  \label{lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections}
1736	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections}
1737	  \uses{lem:gf_finite_sections_of_basicOpen_finite_cover,
1738	        lem:gf_finiteType_affine_finite_cover_generated,
1739	        lem:gf_qcoh_finite_sections_globally_generated}

1741	  % the abstract SheafOfModules.IsFiniteType / IsQuasicoherent local-generator
1742	  % predicates, not this affine-local finiteness.
1743	  % NOTE: overlaps the QUOT keystone lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen — to be
1744	  % extracted to shared qcoh-affine-local infra (see STRATEGY Open questions).
1745	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", Tag 01PB
1746	  % (Lemma "Characterizing modules of finite type", \S "Characterizing modules of
1747	  % finite type and finite presentation")
1748	  % (read from references/stacks-properties.tex, L2092-L2098).
1749	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1750	  % "Let $X = \Spec(R)$ be an affine scheme.
1751	  %  The quasi-coherent sheaf of $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules
1752	  %  $\widetilde M$ is a finite type $\mathcal{O}_X$-module
1753	  %  if and only if $M$ is a finite $R$-module."
1754	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", Tag 01PB.}
1755	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(\F : X.\mathrm{Modules}\) a quasi-coherent sheaf of
1756	  modules with \(\F\) of finite type. Then for every affine open \(W \subseteq X\), the
1757	  section module \(\Gamma(\F, W)\) is a finite module over \(\Gamma(X, W)\).
1758	\end{lemma}
1759	
1760	\begin{proof}
1761	  \uses{lem:gf_finite_sections_of_basicOpen_finite_cover,
1762	        lem:gf_finiteType_affine_finite_cover_generated,
1763	        lem:gf_qcoh_finite_sections_globally_generated}
1764	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the comment above the statement (Tag 01PB, L2092-L2098;
1765	  % the Stacks proof reduces finite-type on a standard open cover to the algebra
1766	  % gluing lemma for finite generation of $M$ from finite generation of each $M_{f_i}$).
1767	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", Tag 01PB.}
1768	  Fix an affine open \(W \subseteq X\). Cover extraction
1769	  \cref{lem:gf_finiteType_affine_finite_cover_generated} gives a finite
1770	  \(t \subseteq \Gamma(X, W)\) spanning the unit ideal, with each \(D(g)\) (\(g \in t\))
1771	  affine and \(\F|_{D(g)}\) globally generated by finitely many sections. The base case
1772	  \cref{lem:gf_qcoh_finite_sections_globally_generated} applied on each affine \(D(g)\)
1773	  makes \(\Gamma(\F, D(g))\) finite over \(\Gamma(X, D(g))\). The locality reduction
1774	  \cref{lem:gf_finite_sections_of_basicOpen_finite_cover} then glues these per-member
1775	  finiteness statements to give \(\Gamma(\F, W)\) finite over \(\Gamma(X, W)\).
1776	\end{proof}

1778	\begin{lemma}[G3 — flat-locality assembly from per-patch freeness]
1779	  \label{lem:gf_flat_locality_assembly}
1780	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_flat_locality_assembly}
1781	  \uses{lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections}
1782	  % LEAN STATUS: G3 — project-built; the geometric glue identifying Γ(F, W∩W_j)
1783	  % localisations across the source cover and the base-restriction is the missing
1784	  % plumbing. Stub-level this iter; built after G1.
1785	  Let \(p : X \to S\) be a finite-type morphism with \(S\) integral and locally
1786	  noetherian, and \(\F\) a finite-type quasi-coherent \(\OO_X\)-module. Fix a non-empty
1787	  affine open \(U_0 = \Spec A \subseteq S\) with \(A\) a noetherian domain, a finite
1788	  affine cover \(\{W_j = \Spec B_j\}_j\) of \(p^{-1}(U_0)\), and an element
1789	  \(f \in A\), \(f \ne 0\), such that for every \(j\) the localized section module
1790	  \((M_j)_f\) (with \(M_j = \Gamma(\F, W_j)\)) is free over \(A_f\). Set
1791	  \(V = D(f) \subseteq U_0\). Then for every affine open \(U \le V\) of \(S\) and every
1792	  affine open \(W \le p^{-1}(U)\) of \(X\), the section module \(\Gamma(\F, W)\) is flat
1793	  over \(\Gamma(S, U)\).
1794	\end{lemma}
1795	
1796	\begin{proof}
1797	  \uses{lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections}
1798	  Free modules are flat (\(\OO\)-flatness of a free module), so each \((M_j)_f\) is flat
1799	  over \(A_f\). Flatness of a module is local on the source: a module is flat over a
1800	  ring iff it is flat after localizing at every maximal ideal, and the family
1801	  \(\{W_j\}\) covers \(p^{-1}(U_0)\), so the per-patch flatness statements assemble to
1802	  flatness of the section module of \(\F\) over \(A_f = \Gamma(S, V)\) on the whole
1803	  preimage \(p^{-1}(V)\). The flat locus is moreover stable under base-restriction along
1804	  the open immersion \(U \hookrightarrow V\): for an affine \(U \le V\) the ring
1805	  \(\Gamma(S, U)\) is a localization of \(\Gamma(S, V) = A_f\) (an open immersion of
1806	  affines into a basic open being a localization), and for an affine \(W \le p^{-1}(U)\)
1807	  the module \(\Gamma(\F, W)\) is the corresponding localization of the section module
1808	  over the relevant patch \(W_j\) (via \cref{lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections}
1809	  identifying the finite section modules and their localizations across the cover).
1810	  Flatness is preserved under localization of both the ring and the module, so
1811	  \(\Gamma(\F, W)\) is flat over \(\Gamma(S, U)\), as claimed.
1812	\end{proof}

1824	  % NOTE (re-signed): the Lean signature `AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatness`

1835	  %                            Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.Quasicoherent)

1841	  %       (p : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType p] [QuasiCompact p] (F : X.Modules)
1842	  %       [F.IsQuasicoherent] [F.IsFiniteType] :
1843	  %       ∃ (V : S.Opens), (V : Set S).Nonempty ∧ (the fibrewise flatness conclusion,
1844	  %       quantified over affine opens U ≤ V of S and affine opens W ≤ p⁻¹U of X, with
1845	  %       Γ(F,W) given its Γ(S,U)-module structure via p.appLE — verbatim form in the
1846	  %       .lean file, the source of truth).
1847	  %
1848	  % NOTE (iter-023): `[QuasiCompact p]` is essential — LocallyOfFiniteType does NOT
1849	  % entail QuasiCompact; without it the statement is false (counterexample:
1850	  % ⊔_i Spec ℤ → Spec ℤ with a finite-type torsion sheaf). It is the extra clause
1851	  % promoting `LocallyOfFiniteType` to `FiniteType`, and there is no instance
1852	  % `LocallyOfFiniteType → QuasiCompact`. The header above carries it, and the Step-1
1853	  % proof prose below invokes it directly (no false "finite type ⟹ quasi-compact" step).
1854	
1855	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4, Theorem ``generic flatness''
1856	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1781-L1787).
1857	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1858	  % "Let \(S\) be a noetherian and integral scheme.
1859	  %  Let \(p: X\to S\) be a finite type morphism, and let \(\F\) be a
1860	  %  coherent sheaf of \({\mathcal O}_X\)-modules. Then there exists a non-empty
1861	  %  open subscheme \(U\subset S\) such that the restriction of \(\F\)
1862	  %  to \(X_U = p^{-1}(U)\) is flat over \({\mathcal O}_U\)."
1863	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, Theorem ``generic flatness''.}
1864	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian integral scheme, \(p : X \to S\) a finite-type morphism,
1865	  and \(\F\) a coherent \(\OO_X\)-module. Then there exists a non-empty open
1866	  subscheme \(U \subseteq S\) such that the restriction of \(\F\) to
1867	  \(X_U = p^{-1}(U)\) is \(\OO_U\)-flat.
1868	
1869	  The coherence hypothesis on \(\F\) is essential: a non-coherent \(\OO_X\)-module
1870	  need not become flat over any non-empty open of \(S\).
1871	\end{theorem}
1872	
1873	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "The above theorem has the following consequence, which
1874	% follows by restricting attention to a non-empty affine open subscheme of \(S\)."
1875	% (Source: nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1776-L1777. The
1876	% Nitsure proof is the single sentence quoted: deduce the geometric form from
1877	% the algebraic generic flatness statement (\cref{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic})
1878	% by restricting to a non-empty
1879	% affine open of \(S\), where the situation reduces to the algebraic statement.)
1880	\begin{proof}
1881	  \uses{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic, lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections,
1882	        lem:gf_flat_locality_assembly}
1883	  Restrict to a non-empty affine open \(U_0 = \Spec A \subseteq S\), where \(A\) is a
1884	  noetherian domain (\(S\) integral and locally noetherian). We assemble the witness
1885	  \(V\) from the algebraic form on a finite affine cover.
1886	
1887	  \emph{Step 1 — finite affine cover above \(U_0\).} Since \(p\) is quasi-compact (by
1888	  the \([\mathrm{QuasiCompact}\,p]\) hypothesis) and \(U_0\) is affine hence
1889	  quasi-compact, the preimage \(X_{U_0} = p^{-1}(U_0)\) is quasi-compact; choose a
1890	  finite affine cover \(\{W_j = \Spec B_j\}_{j}\) of \(X_{U_0}\) by affine opens of
1891	  \(X\). Because \(p\) is locally of finite type, each ring \(B_j = \Gamma(X, W_j)\)
1892	  is a finite-type \(A\)-algebra.
1893	
1894	  \emph{Step 2 — read off a finite module on each patch.} On each \(W_j\), quasi-coherence
1895	  of \(\F\) identifies \(\F|_{W_j}\) with the associated sheaf \(\widetilde{M_j}\) of the
1896	  section module \(M_j = \Gamma(\F, W_j)\), and the finite-section bridge
1897	  \cref{lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections} — applied to the affine open \(W_j\) — makes
1898	  \(M_j\) a finite \(B_j\)-module. Through the scalar tower \(A \to B_j \to M_j\),
1899	  \(M_j\) is the data fed to the algebraic form.
1900	
1901	  \emph{Step 3 — apply the algebraic form and take the common basic open.}
1902	  \cref{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic} applied to \((A, B_j, M_j)\) yields \(f_j \in A\),
1903	  \(f_j \ne 0\), with \((M_j)_{f_j}\) free over \(A_{f_j}\). As the cover is finite, set
1904	  \(f := \prod_j f_j \ne 0\) (a non-zero element of the domain \(A\)) and
1905	  \(V := D(f) \subseteq \Spec A \subseteq S\), a non-empty basic open; over \(V\) every
1906	  \((M_j)_f\) is free over \(A_f\).
1907	
1908	  \emph{Step 4 — freeness implies flatness.} On each patch the localized section module
1909	  is free, hence flat over \(A_f\); the flat-locality assembly
1910	  \cref{lem:gf_flat_locality_assembly} promotes these per-patch freeness statements to
1911	  the fibrewise flatness conclusion, via the criterion that a module flat at every
1912	  maximal ideal is flat together with the locality of flatness on the source cover. This
1913	  produces the required \(V\) with \(\F|_{X_V}\) flat over \(\OO_V\).
1914	\end{proof}

1918	
1919	Mathlib (master \texttt{b80f227}) provides \texttt{Module.Flat}
1920	(\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Flat.Basic}), the module-theoretic notion of
1921	flatness applicable affine-locally to the section modules of \(\F\), together
1922	with the dévissage and Noether-normalisation infrastructure that the algebraic
1923	generic-flatness argument consumes
1924	(\texttt{IsNoetherianRing.induction\_on\_isQuotientEquivQuotientPrime},
1925	\texttt{Module.FinitePresentation.exists\_free\_localizedModule\_powers},
1926	\texttt{exists\_finite\_inj\_algHom\_of\_fg}).  It does \emph{not} yet contain
1927	the polynomial-ring core of generic freeness (a finite module over
1928	\(A[X_1, \dots, X_d]\) becomes free after inverting a single element), nor the
1929	geometric statement \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatness}
1930	(\cref{thm:generic_flatness}) packaging the algebraic form
1931	(\cref{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic}) over a noetherian integral base.  These
1932	are introduced in
1933	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FlatteningStratification.lean}.

2315	\section{Support and freeness predicates}
2316	\label{sec:quot_predicates}
2317	
2318	The Quot functor (\cref{def:quot_functor}) and the Grassmannian
2319	(\cref{def:grassmannian_scheme}) classify quotient sheaves subject to two
2320	geometric conditions: a coherent quotient must have \emph{schematic support
2321	proper over the base}, and the Grassmannian's quotients must be \emph{locally
2322	free of a fixed rank}. This section introduces both predicates.

2346	  %  and let $L$ be a line bundle on $X$. Let $\F$
2347	  %  be any coherent sheaf on
2348	  %  $X$ whose schematic support is proper over $S$."
2349	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
2350	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a sheaf of
2351	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules that is \emph{quasi-coherent and of finite type}
2352	  (over a locally noetherian base, so that on each affine open \(U\) the section
2353	  module \(\mathcal{F}(U)\) is a finitely generated \(\mathcal{O}_X(U)\)-module).
2354	  The \emph{annihilator ideal sheaf} of \(\mathcal{F}\) is the ideal sheaf
2355	  \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})\) defined on affine opens: for each affine open
2356	  \(U \subseteq X\),
2357	  \[
2358	    \mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})(U)
2359	    \;=\; \mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}\bigl(\mathcal{F}(U)\bigr)
2360	    \;\subseteq\; \mathcal{O}_X(U),
2361	  \]
2362	  the annihilator of the \(\mathcal{O}_X(U)\)-module \(\mathcal{F}(U)\). These
2363	  local data patch to a coherent ideal sheaf on \(X\) whose vanishing locus is
2364	  the set-theoretic support of \(\mathcal{F}\).
2365	
2366	  \emph{Well-definedness on basic opens.} The coherence condition for an ideal
2367	  sheaf requires that, for an affine open \(U\) and \(f \in \mathcal{O}_X(U)\),
2368	  the section \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})(U)\) restricts correctly to the basic
2369	  open \(D(f) \subseteq U\), i.e. that
2370	  \(\mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(D(f))}(\mathcal{F}(D(f)))\) is the extension of
2371	  \(\mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}(\mathcal{F}(U))\) along the localization map
2372	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(U) \to \mathcal{O}_X(D(f))\). This is exactly where the two
2373	  hypotheses enter. The bridge
2374	  \cref{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen} supplies, for a quasi-coherent
2375	  finite-type \(\mathcal{F}\), that \(\mathcal{O}_X(D(f)) = (\mathcal{O}_X(U))_f\)
2376	  and that the restriction \(\mathcal{F}(U) \to \mathcal{F}(D(f))\) exhibits
2377	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(f))\) as the localized module \((\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1}
2378	  \mathcal{F}(U)\); the algebra engine
2379	  \cref{lem:annihilator_localization_eq_map} then yields, using that
2380	  \(\mathcal{F}(U)\) is finitely generated, the required identity
2381	  \(\mathrm{Ann}_{(\mathcal{O}_X(U))_f}\bigl((\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1}
2382	  \mathcal{F}(U)\bigr) = \mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}(\mathcal{F}(U)) \cdot
2383	  (\mathcal{O}_X(U))_f\). Thus the local annihilators are compatible with basic-open
2384	  restriction, which is the patching datum that makes \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})\)
2385	  a well-defined ideal sheaf. The finite-type hypothesis is genuinely required:
2386	  without finite generation of \(\mathcal{F}(U)\) the annihilator need not commute
2387	  with localization, and the local data fail to patch.
2388	
2389	  \emph{Note.} Mathlib does not carry an annihilator for sheaves of modules on a scheme.
2390	\end{definition}
2391	
2392	\begin{lemma}\leanok
2393	[Annihilator ideal bounded by the module annihilator on affine opens]
2394	  \label{lem:modules_annihilator_ideal_le}
2395	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.annihilator_ideal_le}
2396	  \uses{def:modules_annihilator}
2397	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, \(\mathcal{F}\) a sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules and
2398	  \(U \subseteq X\) an affine open. Then the section over \(U\) of the annihilator
2399	  ideal sheaf \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})\) (\cref{def:modules_annihilator}) is
2400	  contained in the module-annihilator of the sections of \(\mathcal{F}\) over
2401	  \(U\):
2402	  \[
2403	    \mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})(U)
2404	    \;\subseteq\; \mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}\bigl(\mathcal{F}(U)\bigr).
2405	  \]
2406	  This is the inclusion direction available directly from the definition of
2407	  \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})\); the reverse inclusion is the basic-open coherence
2408	  statement gated on \cref{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen}. Proved directly
2409	  in Lean. It supports the well-definedness of the schematic support
2410	  (\cref{def:schematic_support}).
2411	\end{lemma}
2412	
2413	\begin{lemma}\leanok
2414	[Annihilator family is basic-open coherent on affine opens]
2415	  \label{lem:annihilator_map_basicOpen}
2416	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.annihilator_map_basicOpen}
2417	  \uses{def:modules_annihilator, lem:annihilator_localization_eq_map,
2418	    lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen}
2419	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules,
2420	  \(V \subseteq X\) an affine open, and \(f \in \mathcal{O}_X(V)\), and suppose the section
2421	  module \(\mathcal{F}(V)\) is \emph{finitely generated} over \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\). Then the
2422	  module-annihilator family is coherent across the basic open \(D(f) \subseteq V\): the
2423	  extension of \(\mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(V)}(\mathcal{F}(V))\) along the restriction map
2424	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(V) \to \mathcal{O}_X(D(f))\) equals the module-annihilator over \(D(f)\),
2425	  \[
2426	    \bigl(\mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(V)}(\mathcal{F}(V))\bigr)\cdot \mathcal{O}_X(D(f))
2427	    \;=\; \mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(D(f))}\bigl(\mathcal{F}(D(f))\bigr).
2428	  \]
2429	  This is exactly the \(\mathrm{map\_ideal\_basicOpen}\) coherence field needed to assemble the
2430	  annihilator local data into an \(\mathrm{IdealSheafData}\) (\cref{lem:modules_annihilator_ideal}).
2431	\end{lemma}
2432	\begin{proof}
2433	  \uses{lem:annihilator_localization_eq_map, lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen}
2434	  Since \(V\) is affine, \(\mathcal{O}_X(D(f)) = (\mathcal{O}_X(V))_f\) is the localization at the
2435	  powers of \(f\), and by \cref{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen} the restriction
2436	  \(\mathcal{F}(V) \to \mathcal{F}(D(f))\) exhibits \(\mathcal{F}(D(f))\) as the localized module
2437	  \((\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1}\mathcal{F}(V)\). As \(\mathcal{F}(V)\) is finitely generated,
2438	  \cref{lem:annihilator_localization_eq_map} identifies the annihilator of the localized module with
2439	  the extension of \(\mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(V)}(\mathcal{F}(V))\) along the structure map. This is
2440	  the asserted equality.
2441	\end{proof}
2442	
2443	\begin{lemma}\leanok
2444	[Annihilator ideal sheaf agrees with the module annihilator on affine opens]
2445	  \label{lem:modules_annihilator_ideal}
2446	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.annihilator_ideal}
2447	  \uses{def:modules_annihilator, lem:modules_annihilator_ideal_le,
2448	    lem:annihilator_map_basicOpen}
2449	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules,
2450	  and suppose the section module \(\mathcal{F}(V)\) is \emph{finitely generated} over
2451	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\) at \emph{every} affine open \(V \subseteq X\). Then at every affine open
2452	  \(U \subseteq X\) the section over \(U\) of the annihilator ideal sheaf
2453	  \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})\) (\cref{def:modules_annihilator}) \emph{equals} the
2454	  module-annihilator of the sections:
2455	  \[
2456	    \mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})(U)
2457	    \;=\; \mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}\bigl(\mathcal{F}(U)\bigr).
2458	  \]
2459	  The global finiteness hypothesis is necessary: \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})\) is the
2460	  \(\mathrm{IdealSheafData.ofIdeals}\) of the local annihilator data, i.e.\ the \emph{largest
2461	  coherent} ideal sheaf dominated by that family (not the naive section-wise infimum), so reading
2462	  off its value at one \(U\) requires the family to cohere at every affine open — exactly the
2463	  global condition, in parallel with Mathlib's \(\mathrm{Hom.ker\_apply}\) (global
2464	  \(\mathrm{QuasiCompact}\)). For a \emph{finite-type} quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) the
2465	  hypothesis is supplied at every affine \(V\) by the G1 affine-section finiteness lemma
2466	  (\cref{lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections}, finite type being affine-local), so the finite-type
2467	  form is the specialisation that discharges the hypothesis once G1 lands.
2468	\end{lemma}
2469	\begin{proof}
2470	  \uses{lem:modules_annihilator_ideal_le, lem:annihilator_map_basicOpen}
2471	  The forward inclusion \(\subseteq\) at each \(U\) is \cref{lem:modules_annihilator_ideal_le}. For
2472	  the equality we assemble the local annihilator data into a genuine ideal sheaf and read off its
2473	  value. The family \(V \mapsto \mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(V)}(\mathcal{F}(V))\) satisfies the
2474	  basic-open coherence condition \(\mathrm{map\_ideal\_basicOpen}\) at every affine open, by
2475	  \cref{lem:annihilator_map_basicOpen} (using the finiteness hypothesis at each \(V\)); hence it
2476	  defines an \(X.\mathrm{IdealSheafData}\) \(I\) with \(I(V) = \mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(V)}(\mathcal{F}(V))\).
2477	  By construction \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F}) = \mathrm{ofIdeals}(I)\), and
2478	  \(\mathrm{ofIdeals\_ideal}\) gives \(\mathrm{ofIdeals}(I).\mathrm{ideal} = I.\mathrm{ideal}\).
2479	  Evaluating at the affine open \(U\) yields
2480	  \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})(U) = I(U) = \mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}(\mathcal{F}(U))\),
2481	  simultaneously at every affine \(U\).
2482	\end{proof}
2483	
2484	\begin{lemma}\leanok
2485	[Annihilator of a finitely generated module commutes with
2486	  localization]
2487	  \label{lem:annihilator_localization_eq_map}
2488	  \lean{Module.annihilator_isLocalizedModule_eq_map}
2489	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, let \(S \subseteq R\) be a multiplicative
2490	  submonoid, and let \(R_S = S^{-1}R\) be the localization of \(R\) at \(S\). Let
2491	  \(M\) be a \emph{finitely generated} \(R\)-module and let \(f : M \to M_S\) be
2492	  an \(R\)-linear map exhibiting \(M_S\) as the localized module \(S^{-1}M\)
2493	  (i.e.\ \(f\) is a localization of \(M\) at \(S\)). Then the annihilator of
2494	  \(M_S\) over \(R_S\) is the extension of the annihilator of \(M\) along the
2495	  structure map \(R \to R_S\):
2496	  \[
2497	    \mathrm{Ann}_{R_S}(M_S)
2498	    \;=\; (\mathrm{Ann}_R M)\cdot R_S
2499	    \;=\; \mathrm{map}\bigl(R \to R_S\bigr)\bigl(\mathrm{Ann}_R M\bigr).
2500	  \]
2501	  Equivalently, for finitely generated \(M\) the annihilator commutes with
2502	  localization: \(\mathrm{Ann}(S^{-1}M) = S^{-1}\,\mathrm{Ann}(M)\).
2503	
2504	  \emph{The finite-generation hypothesis is essential.} For a module that is not
2505	  finitely generated the inclusion \(S^{-1}\,\mathrm{Ann}(M) \subseteq
2506	  \mathrm{Ann}(S^{-1}M)\) can be strict, so the identity fails. This is the
2507	  standard commutative-algebra fact that the annihilator of a finite module
2508	  localizes; it is recorded here as the load-bearing algebra engine for the
2509	  basic-open coherence of \cref{def:modules_annihilator}.
2510	\end{lemma}
2511	
2512	\begin{proof}
2513	  \(\supseteq\): if \(r \in \mathrm{Ann}_R M\), then \(r\) kills every element of
2514	  \(M\), hence its image \(r/1 \in R_S\) kills every element of \(M_S\) (each such
2515	  element is \(m/s = (1/s)\cdot f(m)\) for some \(m \in M\), \(s \in S\), and
2516	  \((r/1)\cdot(m/s) = (1/s)\cdot f(r\,m) = 0\)). Thus
2517	  \((\mathrm{Ann}_R M)\cdot R_S \subseteq \mathrm{Ann}_{R_S}(M_S)\).
2518	
2519	  \(\subseteq\): write \(M = \langle m_1, \dots, m_n\rangle\) using the
2520	  finite-generation hypothesis, and let \(y \in \mathrm{Ann}_{R_S}(M_S)\). Write
2521	  \(y = a/s\) with \(a \in R\), \(s \in S\). For each generator \(m_i\) the element
2522	  \(y\cdot f(m_i) = (a/s)\cdot f(m_i) = 0\) in \(M_S\); by the definition of the
2523	  localized module this means there is \(u_i \in S\) with \(u_i\,a\,m_i = 0\) in
2524	  \(M\). Put \(u = \prod_{i=1}^n u_i \in S\). Then \(u\,a\) kills every generator
2525	  \(m_i\), hence kills all of \(M\), so \(u\,a \in \mathrm{Ann}_R M\). Finally
2526	  \(y = a/s = (u\,a)/(u\,s)\) lies in \(S^{-1}\,\mathrm{Ann}_R M =
2527	  (\mathrm{Ann}_R M)\cdot R_S\). Therefore \(\mathrm{Ann}_{R_S}(M_S) \subseteq
2528	  (\mathrm{Ann}_R M)\cdot R_S\), and combined with the reverse inclusion the two
2529	  ideals coincide. The use of a single common multiplier \(u = \prod_i u_i\) is
2530	  exactly the step that requires finitely many generators.
2531	\end{proof}
2532	
2533	\begin{lemma}[Sections on a basic open localize the affine ring]
2534	  \label{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}
2535	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_basicOpen}
2536	  \mathlibok
2537	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
2538	  (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme}).}
2539	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, \(U \subseteq X\) an affine open, and
2540	  \(f \in \Gamma(X, U)\). Then the restriction map
2541	  \(\Gamma(X, U) \to \Gamma\bigl(X,\, D(f)\bigr)\) on the basic open
2542	  \(D(f) = X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,f\) exhibits \(\Gamma(X, D(f))\) as the
2543	  localization of \(\Gamma(X, U)\) away from \(f\); that is, it is
2544	  \(\mathrm{IsLocalization.Away}\,f\), so
2545	  \(\Gamma(X, D(f)) = (\Gamma(X, U))_f\) as a \(\Gamma(X,U)\)-algebra.
2546	\end{lemma}
2547	
2548	\begin{lemma}\leanok
2549	[Quasi-coherent sections on a basic open are the localized module]
2550	  \label{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen}
2551	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen}
2552	  \uses{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib, lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization,
2553	    lem:qcoh_affine_isIso_fromTildeΓ,
2554	    lem:isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ,
2555	    lem:qcoh_pullback_fromSpec, lem:modules_restrict_linear,
2556	    lem:modules_restrict_basicOpen_linear, lem:fromSpec_image_top_section_coherence,
2557	    lem:section_localization_hfr_aux_general, lem:isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_hP1}
2558	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Schemes" (chapter tag 020J), \S 7
2559	  % "Quasi-coherent sheaves on affines", Lemma \texttt{lemma-spec-sheaves},

Now let me check the key points about the `lem:gf_affine_qcoh_Gamma_epi` proof. The directive mentions that the Γ-epi descent must NOT rely on a false bare stalkwise-epi⟹global-epi step. Let me reread the seam 2 part (I already have it in the reading above):

From what I've read:
```
\begin{lemma}[Half 2, seam 2 — $\Gamma$ on affine quasi-coherent modules sends epis to surjections]
```

The key insight is that over an affine scheme, the global sections functor establishes an equivalence between quasi-coherent sheaves and modules over the coordinate ring. Since affine schemes have no higher quasi-coherent cohomology, any sheaf epimorphism gets carried to a surjective module homomorphism under global sections. So the image of the epimorphism π under Γ is indeed surjective.

# Blueprint Review — iter-047
**Date:** 2026-06-09  
**Reviewer:** blueprint-reviewer subagent  
**Scope:** All chapters in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (7 chapters audited: 6 read, 1 unreadable)

---

## HARD-GATE VERDICTS

### 1. `Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex` — GF G1 base case

**verdict: complete=true, correct=true — HARD GATE PASSED**

- `lem:gf_finiteType_affine_finite_cover_generated` (seam 1): complete+correct. Routes through quasi-compactness of affine W + finite-type of F → finite open cover of globally-generated affines. No stalkwise argument. `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_finiteType_affine_finite_cover_generated}` present. No `\uses{}` needed (Mathlib-only dependencies). ✓
- `lem:gf_affine_qcoh_Gamma_epi` (seam 2): complete+**correct**. Γ-epi descent explicitly routes through affine-qcoh exactness of Γ (QCoh≃Mod equivalence, the exact functor — Stacks 01PB), **NOT** a stalkwise-epi⟹global-epi step. `\uses{lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization}` — label exists in `Picard_QuotScheme.tex:3166` ✓. This is the critical seam; math is sound.
- `lem:gf_qcoh_finite_sections_globally_generated` (seam 3): complete+correct. Uses seam 2 surjection + module-finite-of-surjective. `\uses{}` lists `lem:gf_affine_qcoh_Gamma_epi` and `lem:module_finite_of_surjective_mathlib` — both labels exist (QuotScheme:901) ✓.
- `lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections` (G1 assembly): complete+correct. Three-seam chain: seam 1 → seam 3 → locality half. `\uses{lem:gf_finite_sections_of_basicOpen_finite_cover, lem:gf_finiteType_affine_finite_cover_generated, lem:gf_qcoh_finite_sections_globally_generated}` — all labels verified ✓. Lean target has `[F.IsQuasicoherent] [F.IsFiniteType]` hypotheses ✓.
- `lem:gf_flat_locality_assembly` (G3 stub): **MUST-FIX (see below)** — proof prose is informal/vague; the "locality of flatness on source cover" step and section-module identification across the cover are not justified at the level required for prover dispatch. G3 is not a hard gate this iter but blocks `thm:generic_flatness` prover dispatch.
- Pre-existing NOTE in `lem:gf_polynomial_core`: "L5 OreLocalization instance blocker — do NOT re-dispatch raw L5 prover round without instance-alignment refactor." This is a Lean elaboration issue already documented in the blueprint; no blueprint correction needed.

### 2. `Picard_SectionGradedRing.tex` — SNAP 3-layer infrastructure

**verdict: complete=true, correct=true — HARD GATE PASSED**

- Layer 1 (tensor powers): `def:sheafTensorObj`, `def:sheafTensorPow`, `def:sheafModuleTwist`, `lem:sheafTensorPow_add` — all have complete proofs. `def:sheafTensorObj` buildability chain: objectwise tensor on `PresheafOfModules` (via `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory` ✓ in Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Monoidal) + sheafification (via `PresheafOfModules.sheafification` ✓) + unit (via `SheafOfModules.unit` ✓ in Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Modules/Tilde.lean). - Layer 3 (graded assembly): `lem:sectionGradedRing_gcommSemiring`, `lem:sectionGradedModule_gmodule` — complete proofs. `DirectSum.GCommSemiring` ✓ (Mathlib.Algebra.DirectSum.Ring), `DirectSum.Gmodule` / `Gmodule` class ✓ (Mathlib.Algebra.Module.GradedModule) — both `\mathlibok` anchors are real Mathlib declarations.

### 3. `Picard_QuotScheme.tex` — annihilator section (iter-046/047 rewrite)

- `lem:annihilator_map_basicOpen` (NEW): `\leanok` ✓. Uses LOCAL finiteness at V. Proves basic-open coherence. Routes through `lem:annihilator_localization_eq_map` + `lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen` (label exists at QuotScheme:2550 ✓). Correct.
- `lem:modules_annihilator_ideal`: `\leanok` ✓. - `lem:annihilator_localization_eq_map`: `\lean{Module.annihilator_isLocalizedModule_eq_map}` ✓. Complete proof. Correct.
